Note: New issues with the pan temperatur.label: Note: New issues with the pan temperature is not cold

technician.text: If customer complains that the pan temperature is too high or uneven. Below are the recommended solutions:

steps.text: Replace with a larger compressor W0201043-NUT70NR1.
Modify the parameters; set Hy to 5.
Modify the parameters; set id to 5.
Please make sure that the Air Divider, Insert, Pan, and Base Support must not be removed under any circumstances.

customer.text: Ask the customer to record a video and send it to us for analysis.
customer.red: (Make sure to upload it into the system)

customer.text: Check if the condenser fan blade is hitting any foreign objects.

technician.text: Check if noise outside:
steps: Check if the fan motor is defective.
Check whether there is any noise caused by loose screws.
Inspect the compressor for abnormal noise.

customer.text: Ask the customer to take a picture and send it to us for analysis.
customer.red: (Make sure to upload it into the system)

customer.text: Confirm whether the bottom bracket has been removed.
customer.red: This part is only to prevent the door from sagging during transport; it must be removed during use to avoid air infiltration and excessive condensation.

customer.text: Is the Unit Level?
customer.red: (A unit not being level will cause a puddle to form on a side of the unit rather than just in the back of the unit)

customer.text: Drain Hose Clogged?
customer.red: (Scale build up is the most likely cause of leaking within the unit. If customer is comfortable, this issue is fixable over the phone by explaining how to remove and clean the drain pipe)

customer.text: Is there any hot equipment placed next to the refrigerator?
customer.red: (The temperature and heat from nearby hot products can directly affect the refrigerator's surface or cause excess moisture inside. This is a normal occurrence, unless the customer keeps the hot equipment far away from the refrigerator.)

customer.text: If the door gasket is damaged, suggest the customer replace it themselves.
customer.red: Within warranty → part covered; customer self-install; no technician dispatch. (Door gaskets are considered consumable parts.) Send the replacement tutorial video to the customer.

customer.text: Ask the customer to record a picture and send it to us for analysis.
customer.red: (Make sure to upload it into the system)
steps: If condensation forms on the top panel, keep the top clear (no items placed).
Explain that in high-humidity environments, this problem is more likely to occur.

customer.text: Is there any hot equipment placed next to the refrigerator?
customer.red: (The temperature and heat from nearby hot products can directly affect the refrigerator's surface or cause excess moisture inside. This is a normal occurrence, unless the customer keeps the hot equipment far away from the refrigerator.)

technician.text: Surface Condensation on Unit?
technician.red: If the back/side leakage is heavy condensate, have the tech adhere insulation foam/insulation cotton to those areas. (Require the tech to document ambient temp & RH and upload photos for our records)

technician.text: There's a lot of condensation under the countertop?
technician.red: Advise the technician to apply insulation cotton/foam at the joints to prevent cold-air leakage.

technician.text: Wire Harness not Sealed?
technician.red: (This occurs when the back right wire harness in the unit is not sealed properly. This appears as a small puddle sourcing near the rear right wheel)

technician.text: Lower suction tube leaking water?
technician.red: (This issue appears as a small trickle or puddle in the back of the unit and is visible when a tech checks the lowside insulation)

technician.text: Condenser drain pan overflow?
technician.red: Send new wicking paper + condensing fan run continuously.

technician.text: Ask the customer to take a photo to confirm if anything is installed on the right side of the unit.
technician.red: Screws might damage the internal wiring, causing the display to be irreparable. (Please remind the customer that after the repair, they'll see a new wiring harness)

technician.text: Check if the display port has a 24V output?

steps.text: No Voltage: Measure the control board's display terminal to check if there is a 24V output. If there is no voltage, the control board needs to be replaced.
Terminal Connection: Inspect if the terminal inside the cabinet is not properly connected.

technician.text: If the new display is still not showing and the voltage is normal.
technician.red: Try reversing the direction of the green clip terminal block and test again. It is possible that the positive and negative connections are reversed.

customer.text: Ask the customer to record a picture and send it to us for analysis.
customer.red: (Make sure to upload it into the system)

customer.text: Lid damaged — customer to replace.
customer.red: Within warranty → part covered; customer self-install; no technician dispatch.

customer.text: If the door gasket is damaged, suggest the customer replace it themselves.
customer.red: Within warranty → part covered; customer self-install; no technician dispatch. (Door gaskets are considered consumable parts.) Send the replacement tutorial video to the customer.

customer.text: The Air Divider weld point is cracked.
customer.red: (Send a new part to the customer, allowing them to install it themselves or arrange for a technician to perform the repair)

link.label: MSF Inner Pan Broken Corner Welds Repair

customer.text: How to replace the Pilasters and Clips?
customer.red: (Send the parts to the customer for self-replacement)

customer.text: If it is clearly a transportation damage issue caused by the customer during handling.
customer.red: (We will only send parts to the customer, not dispatch a technician)

technician.text: If the caster is broken, technician will need to replace it onsite.
If the handle is broken, do NOT send the new Lid! Technician needs to be dispatched for replacement.
technician.red: (Cut the silicone seal on both sides of the lid, then separate the cover plate to access the internal screws. Reminder: After reassembling, remember to reseal with silicone.)

technician.text: If there is an issue with the door, pay attention to the following when replacing it:
technician.red: (When you install the new door, try opening and closing it to see if it closes by itself. If not, then it is not turned correctly or the resistance is not strong enough)

customer.text: Confirm with the customer which icons are displayed on the screen.
customer.red: (The icons indicate that the corresponding components are functioning)

customer.text: Is the condenser fan working?
steps: If not, check if the fan blade is obstructed by any foreign objects.
If it's not obstructed but still not working, the fan needs to be replaced.

customer.text: Check if the internal evaporator fan is working?
customer.red: If only one fan is not working, replace the faulty fan.

technician.text: Check if Evap fans working inside — Please confirm whether the fan voltage is 115V or 24V.
technician.red: (The old unit, which has a transformer, uses a 24V fan, while the updated unit uses a 115V fan)

technician.text: 115V Fan Troubleshooting:

steps.text: If all fans are not working, please check if there is output 115V for the fans on the controller board.
steps.red: (If there is no voltage, replace the controller board)

steps.text: If only one fan is not working, replace the fan directly.

technician.text: 24V Fan Troubleshooting:

steps.text: If all fans are not working, check if the transformer has 115V input or 24V output: ① If the transformer has 115V input but no 24V output, replace the transformer. ② If the transformer has no 115V input, please check if there is a 115V output on the control board.
steps.red: (If there is no voltage, replace the controller board)

technician.text: All parts are functioning properly. Please check if there is a refrigerant leak.
steps: Recommend the technician to test the low-side; the normal low pressure should be 36-38 PSI.

If the tech needs us to provide capillary tube fittings, please let the tech know we recommend cutting 2-3 inches of the end cap tube and blowing the whole system with nitrogen, then replace a new universal filter drier, vacuum and recharge the unit.

technician.text: Is there any hot equipment placed next to the refrigerator?
technician.red: (The temperature and heat from nearby hot products can directly affect the refrigerator's surface or cause excess moisture inside. This is a normal occurrence, unless the customer keeps the hot equipment far away from the refrigerator)

customer.text: The unit is completely non-functional?
customer.red: (Check if the display and condenser fan are completely non-functional. Please replace the power outlet and try again to ensure there is power in the outlet.)

customer.text: Please ask the customer to check if the power cord is damaged?
customer.red: (If there is any damage, please take a photo and send it to us. We will send the part and dispatch a tech to repair it.)

technician.text: MGF, MSF series – Door to Drawer Replacement Video
Dixell Parameters — (All US under-counter refrigerators use the Dixell controller)
steps: P1: Room Probe Failure (White Probe) or Loose Wiring on the controller.
P2: This unit doesn't have a defrost probe. Please calibrate the parameters first (Check the P2 parameter to see if it's set to 'n') or replace the control board.
dA: The MSF series doesn't have door magnetic switches.

technician.text: It is recommended to verify all the parameters based on the parameter table in the document section.
Confirm whether the wiring is correct.

link.label: Please refer to the wiring method of Dixell on Page 5 of 7

technician.text: Carel Parameters — (All Canadian under-counter refrigerators use the Carel controller)
steps: El: Probe 2 Failure (Blue Probe); [dO = 0/1] If refrigerator has El, check parameter dO to 2.
Do: The MSF series doesn't have door magnetic switches.
ER: MSF product, please replace it with the Dixell three-piece set: Front display, green clip, controller board.

technician.text: It is recommended to verify all the parameters based on the parameter table in the document section.
Confirm whether the wiring is correct.

link.label: Please refer to the wiring method of Dixell on Page 4 of 7
